problem.title: Maximal Square
problem.description: Given an m×n binary matrix filled with '0's and '1's, find the largest square containing only '1's and return its area.
assumptions: m and n are at least 1.

example.explanation: A 3×3 square of '1's exists in the bottom-right region of rows 0-2 and columns 2-4.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Dynamic Programming [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: Define dp[i][j] as the side length of the largest square whose bottom-right corner is at (i, j). If matrix[i][j] == '1', then dp[i][j] = min(dp[i-1][j], dp[i][j-1], dp[i-1][j-1]) + 1; otherwise it stays 0. The recurrence works because a square is only as large as the smallest square formed by its three neighboring corners. Track the maximum dp value and return its square.

complexity.time: O(mn) — single pass through all cells
complexity.space: O(mn) for the DP table; reducible to O(n) with a 1D array

example_walkthrough.text: At cell (2,4) (0-indexed), matrix[2][4]='1'. dp[2][4] = min(dp[1][4], dp[2][3], dp[1][3]) + 1 = min(2,2,2)+1 = 3. This means a 3×3 square ends here. Area = 3*3 = 9.
